title: Replication, Multi-Region Access, Backup, and Disaster Recovery · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for replication, multi-region access, backup, and disaster recovery and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for RPO, RTO, replication, failover, and restore dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery.
Configure versioning, destination ownership, IAM, KMS, filters, delete-marker behavior, replication status, metrics, and failure remediation.
Evaluate Multi-Region Access Points, latency routing, active-active writes, consistency, replication lag, failover controls, and client endpoint support.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Durability Availability Recovery
steps.instruction:
Order the Durability Availability Recovery S3, CloudFront, and Go storage-delivery evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Durability Availability Recovery.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Durability Availability Recovery assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Durability Availability Recovery, keep Durability Availability Recovery connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 2943 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate replication, independent backup, restore, and failover evidence; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery.

content.text: Order work so Durability Availability Recovery has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

options.text: For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient Disaster Recovery Drill happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Disaster Recovery Drill
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Disaster Recovery Drill: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 5185 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate replication, independent backup, restore, and failover evidence; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Run controlled region, credential, deletion, corruption, dependency, and restore drills with abort, stakeholder communication, integrity checks, and lessons.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Disaster Recovery Drill.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Disaster Recovery Drill, keep Disaster Recovery Drill connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run controlled region, credential, deletion, corruption, dependency, and restore drills with abort, stakeholder communication, integrity checks, and lessons.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Disaster Recovery Drill, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Disaster Recovery Drill precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Disaster Recovery Drill, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, custody, integrity, range, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 5185 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate replication, independent backup, restore, and failover evidence; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Run controlled region, credential, deletion, corruption, dependency, and restore drills with abort, stakeholder communication, integrity checks, and lessons.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Disaster Recovery Drill, challenge the documented misconception with file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: A replication dashboard in a healthy region proves disaster recovery.
